---
title: "Homework 8"
format: 
  html:
    output-file: "index"
embed-resources: true
editor: 
  markdown: 
    wrap: 72
---

```{r}
library(altair)
library(reticulate)
library(tidyverse)
```

## Exercise 1 (Supply and demand)
```{r}
data <- read_csv("swd-lets-practice.csv") 

chart1 <- alt$Chart(data)$
  transform_fold(
    as.list(c("capacity", "demand")),
    as.list(c("variable", "value"))
  )$
  mark_bar()$
  encode(
    x = alt$X("date:T", title = "Date"),
    xOffset = "variable:N",
    y = alt$Y("value:Q", title = "Count"),
    color = alt$Color(
      "variable:N",
      scale = alt$Scale(
        domain = c("demand", "capacity"),
        range = c("lightblue", "orange")  
      )
    )
  )$
  properties(width = 500, height = 300)

chart1


```

```{r}
chart2 <- alt$Chart(data)$
  transform_fold(
    as.list(c("capacity", "demand")),
    as.list(c("variable", "value"))
  )$
  mark_line()$
  encode(
    x = alt$X("date:T", title = "Date"),
    xOffset = "variable:N",
    y = alt$Y("value:Q", title = "Count"),
    color = alt$Color(
      "variable:N",
      scale = alt$Scale(
        domain = c("demand", "capacity"),
        range = c("lightblue", "orange")  
      )
    )
  )$
  properties(width = 500, height = 300)

chart2

```

```{r}
data <- read_csv(
  "swd-lets-practice.csv") |>
  mutate(
    date = as.Date(paste0(date, "-01")), 
    capacity = as.numeric(str_replace_all(capacity, ",", "")),
    demand   = as.numeric(str_replace_all(demand, ",", ""))
  )


chart3 <- alt$Chart(data)$
  transform_fold(
    as.list(c("capacity", "demand")),
    as.list(c("variable", "value"))
  )$
  mark_bar()$
  encode(
    x = alt$X("date:T", title = "Date"),
    xOffset = "variable:N",                 
    y = alt$Y("value:Q", title = "Count"),
    color = alt$Color(
      "variable:N",
      scale = alt$Scale(
        domain = c("demand", "capacity"),
        range = c("lightblue", "orange")
      )
    )
  )$
  properties(width = 500, height = 300)

chart3

```

```{r}
chart3 <- alt$Chart(data)$
  mark_bar()$
  encode(
    x = alt$X("year:O"),
    y = alt$Y("value:Q"),
    color = "variable:N",
    column = "variable:N"
  )$
  transform_fold(
    as.list(c("demand", "capacity")),
    as.list(c("variable", "value"))
  )$
  properties(width = 100, height = 250)

chart3
```

```{r}
chart6 <- alt$Chart(data)$
  mark_line(point = TRUE, color = "#d62728")$
  encode(
    x = "year:O",
    y = "unmet:Q"
  )$
  properties(width = 400, height = 250)

chart6


```

```{r}
Data2 <- data |> mutate(unmet_demand = pmax(demand - capacity, 0))

chart4 <- alt$Chart(Data2)$
  mark_bar()$
  encode(
    x = "year:O",
    y = "value:Q",
    color = alt$Color("variable:N",
                      scale = alt$Scale(
                        domain = c("capacity", "unmet"),
                        range = c("#4d4d4d", "#d62728")
                      ))
  )$
  transform_fold(
    as.list(c("capacity", "unmet")),
    as.list(c("variable", "value"))
  )$
  properties(width = 400, height = 250)

chart4

```



## Exercise 2(Jobs)
```{r}
library(vegabrite)
jobs_url <- 
  "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vega-datasets@2.8.0/data/jobs.json"


#| fig-cap: "This is a caption for the figure."
vl_chart() |>
  vl_mark_point() |>
  vl_pivot("year", groupby = list("job", "sex"), value = "perc") |>
  vl_encode_x("1850:Q") |>
  vl_scale_x(type = "symlog", constant = 0.00001) |>  # note: scale must be set quite small
  vl_encode_y("2000:Q") |>
  vl_scale_y(type = "symlog", constant = 0.00001) |>  # note: scale must be set quite small
  vl_facet_column("sex:N", title = "") |>
  vl_encode_tooltip_array(list("job", "sex", "1850", "2000")) |>
  vl_add_properties(
    title =  list( 
      text = "Percent of people working various jobs",
      subtitle = "Each dot represents the one occupation. Hover to see which occupation it is."
      )) |>
  vl_add_data_url(jobs_url) 
```
```{python}
import altair as alt
import pandas as pd

jobs_url = "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/vega-datasets@2.8.0/data/jobs.json"
df = pd.read_json(jobs_url)

Perc = (
    df.groupby(["year", "sex"], as_index=False)["perc"]
      .sum()
)

chart = (
    alt.Chart(Perc)
    .mark_line(point=True)
    .encode(
        x=alt.X("year:O", title="Year"),
        y=alt.Y("perc:Q", title="Sum of perc"),
        color="sex:N"
    )
    .properties(
        title="Sum of perc by Year and Sex"
    )
)

chart

```




## Exercise 3
